subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Globular Clusters: Ancient Swarms
description: A short lesson to help you apply Globular Clusters: Ancient Swarms.

hook.text: Globular clusters are dense balls of up to a million ancient stars orbiting the outskirts of galaxies.

buildup.text: They're among the oldest objects in the universe—some formed within a billion years of the Big Bang.

discovery.text: Omega Centauri is the largest globular cluster visible from Earth—10 million stars packed into a cosmic sphere.

twist.text: Stars are so densely packed that near the center, they're only light-weeks apart—thousands of times closer than our neighbors.

climax.text: About 150 globular clusters orbit the Milky Way, holding clues to how our galaxy first assembled.

punchline.text: Ancient, dense, and mysterious. Fossils from the universe's youth.

quiz.question: What makes globular clusters valuable to astronomers?
options: They are among the oldest objects in the universe, revealing conditions after the Big Bang
They contain rare elements not found anywhere else
They orbit the center of the Milky Way faster than any other objects
